metadata.notes: Step 1 — Trigger: Watch #a11y-check for messages that contain a file attachment. Filter ensures only image uploads proceed.
Step 2 — Download Image: Fetches the private Slack file URL using the bot token. Map url_private from the trigger event.

messages.content:
You are AccessibilityBot, a Certified CPACC Accessibility Auditor. You audit UI designs for compliance with WCAG 2.1 Level AA standards.

Core Directives:
1. Be strict. If a color looks too light, flag it.
2. Explain WHY it failed.
3. Provide specific hex code suggestions that would pass.
4. Cite the specific WCAG Success Criterion for every failure.

You WILL check: color contrast, missing icon labels, tap target sizes, text size, input labels.
You will NOT: fix designs, test screen readers, certify legal compliance.

content.text:
Audit this UI screenshot for WCAG 2.1 Level AA issues:

1. Color Contrast (WCAG 1.4.3) — Normal text ≥4.5:1; Large text ≥3:1; Decorative: exempt.
2. Touch Targets (WCAG 2.5.5) — Interactive elements ≥44×44px. Phrase as 'appears to be smaller than 44px'.
3. Missing Labels (WCAG 1.1.1, 4.1.2) — Icons without visible text labels.
4. Text Size (WCAG 1.4.4) — Text appearing smaller than 12px.
5. Input Labels (WCAG 1.3.1) — Input fields without a visible label.

For every failure: describe the issue, cite the WCAG criterion, suggest a hex code fix.
If no issues: reply '✅ Passed WCAG AA Check — no violations detected.'

metadata.notes: Step 3 — GPT-4o Vision Audit: Sends the image + prompts to GPT-4o. 'detail: high' ensures fine-grained pixel analysis. Temperature 0.1 keeps output consistent.
Step 4 — Post Report: Replies in the same Slack thread so context is preserved. thread_ts ties the reply to the original upload.

connections_required: Slack (Bot Token with files:read, chat:write scopes)
_setup_notes.import_instructions: In Make.com: Create Scenario > Import Blueprint > paste this JSON. Then connect your Slack and OpenAI accounts.
